interpretation_summary: The replication team created a clear plan and supplied the raw Prolific panel data together with an R script meant to reproduce the preregistered OLS test (NegativeT2 ~ RealisticT1 + SymbolicT1).  However, the execution pipeline never produced analytical output.  During three rebuild cycles the container repeatedly failed: first because the package ‘rio’ was not installed, and later because of a simple copy-paste error in the R script (the line "df <- read.csv(...)df" caused a parse failure).  No coefficient table or CSV result file was ever generated, so the focal comparison with the original b = 4.02 (p < .001) could not be carried out.  Consequently, the replication yields no empirical evidence either for or against the claim.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Moderate alignment – the supplied R code follows the preregistered steps (same variables, OLS model, inclusion of Symbolic threat covariate).  Yet the analysis never ran because of missing dependencies and a syntax error, so the intended method was not actually executed.

deviations.issue_description: R package ‘rio’ absent from Docker image, causing library load failure in first two runs.
deviations.impact: High: halted execution before any data manipulation.

deviations.issue_description: Duplicated read.csv command in script ("df <- read.csv(...)df") produced a parsing error even after package fix.
deviations.impact: High: prevented model estimation and result export.

deviations.issue_description: Hard-coded file path in early script versions did not match mounted directory.
deviations.impact: Medium: contributed to an additional error cycle but was eventually corrected.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Baseline realistic COVID-19 threat positively predicts negative affect one week later, controlling for symbolic threat.
results_comparison.original_results: OLS / SEM path coefficient b = 4.02, p = 0.000 (positive association).
results_comparison.replication_results: No numerical results – analysis script failed to execute; no coefficient or p-value produced.
results_comparison.overall_answer: No – the replication could not be evaluated because the model did not run.

replication_report: Replication inconclusive: code errors and missing packages prevented estimation, so support for the positive effect of realistic threat on later negative affect could not be assessed.

failure_handling.suggestions: Remove duplicate command in replicate_analysis.R (`df <- read.csv(...)` appears twice); run `R CMD check` locally to catch parse errors before container execution.

Add required R packages in replication_info.json or load base `read.csv` instead of relying on ‘rio’; include an automated unit test that loads the data and fits a minimal model to verify dependencies inside the container.

notes: Future interpreters could compute the regression quickly in Python or corrected R to salvage the attempt, but that lies outside the supplied execution record.
